cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Brad tried to make Radha believe by sharing his own experience. So I need to look at the parts where Brad talks about his personal experience with humor. Brad starts by saying he wasn't funny when he was younger but now he can make people laugh. This suggests that he developed his sense of humor over time. He mentions that improving wit and humor is an ongoing process. So the main point here is that humor isn't something you're born with; it can be developed through practice and experience. Looking at the options: A) a sense of humour can be developed – This matches because Brad's experience shows he improved over time, implying it can be developed. B) a sense of humour helps impress others – Brad does mention that people remember you for humor, but the main focus of his personal experience is about how he developed it, not the effect on others. C) laughter is the best medicine – The passage doesn't talk about health benefits of laughter, so this is probably not the answer.
